summary: Nutrients are principally absorbed in the small intestine because it has an efficient structure for uptake; various studies also note this as the primary site despite initial delivery through the stomach or gut tube. Key literature supports targeting specific intestinal segments like those involved in vitamin B12 and iron absorption post-ingestion. \boxed{}
